functions.role: RCC/clock or DMA channel enable-disable: sets/clears bit1 in a CR reg and reconfigures another reg's bits 12-19 to 0x33 pattern when enabling, else just clears bit1

functions.role: ring-buffer push: writes byte param_1 at buffer+write_index (offset 0x400 holds 16-bit index), increments index mod 0x400 (1024-byte circular buffer) - classic USB-CDC TX/RX ring buffer put
copy 16-bit halfwords from source array into a strided destination buffer (step of 2 units) for param_3+1 words - looks like LCD/display buffer blit, not CDC
pack 5 params (freq/state values) into 14-byte little-endian buffer and pass to FUN_080048b4 (likely USB CDC transmit/queue send)
functions.peripheral: none direct; feeds USB-CDC TX via FUN_080048b4

functions.role: LMX2594 frequency-to-register calculator: range-select N-divider/prescaler table lookup based on target frequency (param_1), computes VCO band/PLL N,FRAC via 64-bit math helpers, writes resulting register words via FUN_080069f4 (SPI1 register-write queue for LMX2594) - core synth programming routine, NOT power-down related
functions.peripheral: SPI1 (indirect, via FUN_080069f4 which is presumed LMX2594 SPI writer)

functions.role: float/double classification helper (checks exponent bits 0x7ff = NaN/Inf, mantissa nonzero) - math library support, unrelated to CDC
ASCII digit-check helper (param-0x30 <10)
empty stub/no-op function
LCD/display bitmap blit: iterates 64 bytes x 8 bits, for each set bit calls FUN_0800278c(pixel-set) with computed x/y from lookup tables - font/char rendering, not CDC

disables SysTick (FUN_080052c0(0xfffffffb)) then computes a scaled byte value (division + multiply-by-approx-126 trick) into DAT_080063a4 - looks like ADC/battery or PWM duty calc, incidental to CDC cluster

SysTick reload/start: writes reload value = param*const to SysTick LOAD (0xe000e014), busy-waits COUNTFLAG (bit16 of CTRL 0xe000e010), clears ENABLE bit, clears VAL (0xe000e018) - blocking millisecond delay routine

MAIN LOOP / command dispatcher: init sequence (delay,SPI/LCD init,LMX2594 init via FUN_08005aac(0/1) + FUN_08004cc4, USB init FUN_08005880+FUN_08005844), then infinite loop reading USB-CDC RX command string (_DAT_08006844 buffer) parsing 'start','stop','w1'..'w3','wt','wv' ASCII commands, updating freq/span/power globals, calling FUN_08005f50 (LMX register calc) + FUN_08003480 (SPI write presumably) + FUN_08006b10 (CDC string TX) + FUN_08005ec4 (status pack) each iteration. NO suspend/idle/low-power branch, NO SPI powerdown call anywhere in this loop.
Main superloop: init sequence then infinite polling loop parsing UART/command strings (start/stop/w1/w2/w3/wt/wv) into freq/power/span state vars, drives sweep + LMX2594 programming
functions.peripheral: none directly; calls SPI/GPIO via subroutines

functions.role: LCD/font bitmap render: iterates 8x8 char bitmap bits from font table, plots pixels via FUN_0800278c depending on threshold table DAT_080069e8 keyed by a param value bucketed into ranges (0-7,8-15,16-23,24-27,28-29 -> pixel offset)
functions.peripheral: none direct (calls low-level pixel plot, likely GPIO bit-bang LCD)

functions.role: Bit-bang serial-out helper: shifts 24 bits of param_1 MSB-first into a GPIO/SPI-like protocol via FUN_080024da/FUN_080024de (set/clear pin), toggling a clock/data or CS pattern 0x800/0x1000/2 masks — looks like manual GPIO bit-bang (chip-select+clock+data) rather than hw SPI, possibly LCD or DDS control
functions.peripheral: GPIO (bit-bang, base not directly visible, uses DAT_08006a68 pointer to a GPIO port ODR/BSRR)

functions.role: SPI1 peripheral init/config for LMX2594: sets CR1 (baud/mode fields 0x400,3,0x18 pattern) then CR2 (0x800,4), configures a struct (local_20..1d = 0x27,1,1,1) via FUN_08003d08 (likely NVIC/DMA), then builds a 12-byte struct with param_1 as field and calls FUN_0800570c/FUN_080056c0(...,0x525,1)/FUN_080055a0 — looks like SPI1 (DAT_08006b08) init + timer/NVIC config (DAT_08006b0c) using param_1=0x2580 as reload/prescaler value
printf-style formatter+UART/LCD char-out: formats param_1..4 via FUN_08000304 (sprintf-like), gets length via FUN_08000540, then streams each byte to FUN_08005bd8 (putchar to UART or LCD)
functions.peripheral: none direct (delegates to FUN_08005bd8 which likely touches USART/GPIO)

functions.role: String compare/lookup helper: if param_2 non-null/non-empty calls FUN_0800113c (strcmp-like) against string at DAT_08006b70+offset, returns 0 on match else returns pointer DAT_08006b74+offset (menu/command table lookup)
Same pattern as FUN_08006b4c, different table offsets (DAT_08006b9c/08006ba0) — another string/command lookup entry
Compiler runtime: IEEE754 double division (__aeabi_ddiv / __divdf3 style), NOT app logic
Runtime helper for double denormalization/rounding, part of same float division library, falls into trap loop on error path
Runtime helper: normalizes an int into double bit pattern (int-to-double conversion, __aeabi_i2d style)
Compiler runtime: IEEE754 double multiplication (__aeabi_dmul / __muldf3)
Float exception/trap dispatcher: jump table on special-value case (NaN/Inf/zero combos), calls into fault handler or does saturating add for edge case
Compiler runtime: IEEE754 double subtraction/addition (__aeabi_dsub/__adddf3), includes bad-instruction trap paths (halt_baddata/software_interrupt 0x40) for exponent overflow
